title: This company is actively hiring
description: Meet Benevity Benevity is the way the world does good, providing companies (and their employees) with technology to take social action on the issues they care about. Through giving, volunteering, grantmaking, employee resource groups and micro-actions, we help most of the Fortune 100 brands build better cultures and use their power for good. We’re also one of the first B Corporations in Canada, meaning we’re as committed to purpose as we are to profits. We have people working all over the world, including Canada, Spain, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the United States and more! High-Level Overview Are you a visionary creative ready to help shape the visual identity of a global brand? As our Creative Director, you will be the guardian of the Benevity brand, responsible for defining and evolving our brand’s visual and digital language to inspire action and drive social impact. You are equally at home concepting a campaign as you are building a project plan — a rare blend of creative and operational leader who thrives on both the big idea and the details that bring it to life. You will work at the intersection of strategy and execution, leading high-stakes creative projects and mentoring designers to elevate our profile as a category leader and the world's enterprise impact platform. This role is ideal for a leader who "grew up digital” and knows how to run a tight creative operation. Managing the processes, contributing to the work and mentoring designers to push their craft, leading high-stakes creative projects, and evolving our brand’s visual language to inspire action and drive global social impact. If you’re ready to take your creative career to the next level, we would love to hear from you! What You'll Do Creative Strategy: Develop and implement a comprehensive brand design strategy aligned with Benevity's business objectives to improve brand equity and visual consistency across all touchpoints. Visual Identity Leadership: Lead the evolution of Benevity’s visual identity system, across all media, ensuring it remains modern, accessible, and deeply resonant with our global audience. Creative Execution: Lead day to day execution and output of creative team and brainstorming for major marketing programs, product launches, and events. Develop a creative base for “big Ideas” that convert product and social impact data into emotional, thumb-stopping creative concepts. Direct, design and produce high-quality creative assets for major campaigns, product launches, and brand initiatives, ensuring excellence in typography, color, engagement (digital) and layout. Operational Leadership & Workload Management: Own the creative team's intake process, project prioritization, and capacity planning to ensure the right work gets done at the right time and that our teams can work at the speed of impact. Build and maintain systems that give the team clarity on priorities, deadlines, and bandwidth — balancing urgent requests against longer-horizon brand-building work without sacrificing quality. Collaboration across the business: Partner with Product, Marketing, Sales and People teams to ensure appropriate input and creative briefs that lend themselves to a unified brand experience across the entire customer and employee journey. Work with and influence stakeholders at all levels, including executives. Web oversight: Partner with the Digital Marketing team to deliver a high-performing, on-brand web experience, establishing and enforcing shared standards that drive improvements in performance, accessibility, and creative consistency. Mentorship & Influence: Provide creative direction and mentorship to the design team, supporting their design time while fostering a culture of innovation, excellence, experimentation and continuous learning. Team and Agency Management: In addition to managing internal team resources, oversee budget and external creative agencies and vendors to ensure timely project execution that meets Benevity’s high standards f
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at purpose-driven global company with Canada presence, but missing salary transparency, unclear remote status, and no explicit work authorization details for Costa Rica residency.

title: Apply with resume & profile
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Organic Growth in Canada. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and strategic marketer who thrives at the intersection of storytelling, customer engagement, and organic growth. In this role, you will shape the voice and emotional connection of a fast-growing consumer platform used by millions of customers. You’ll lead the evolution of lifecycle communications across email, SMS, in-app messaging, and emerging organic channels, transforming transactional interactions into meaningful brand experiences. Working closely with product, growth, design, and customer insights teams, you’ll craft messaging that builds trust, drives retention, and strengthens long-term customer loyalty. This position offers a highly autonomous and collaborative environment where creativity, experimentation, and first-principles thinking are valued. It’s an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys building from the ground up and making a visible impact on brand perception and customer growth. Accountabilities Lead the development and evolution of brand voice, tone, and messaging across lifecycle and organic growth channels. Create and optimize customer communications including email campaigns, SMS messaging, push notifications, in-app copy, onboarding flows, and retention programs. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights to align messaging with user needs and business goals. Build customer trust and engagement through empathetic, human-centered storytelling and value-driven content. Analyze customer feedback, behavioral insights, and qualitative research to improve messaging effectiveness and customer experience. Develop and execute creative organic growth initiatives across channels such as social media, online communities, and content-driven engagement platforms. Contribute to customer retention, brand affinity, and long-term conversion through thoughtful and emotionally resonant communication strategies. Continuously experiment with messaging approaches, audience segmentation, and creative formats to optimize lifecycle performance. Requirements 5+ years of experience in creative marketing, lifecycle marketing, copywriting, or organic growth roles within consumer-focused environments. Strong expertise in writing compelling copy for email, SMS, in-app experiences, social media, and customer engagement campaigns. Proven ability to translate complex or technical topics into clear, engaging, and relatable messaging. Experience building or evolving brand voice and customer communication strategies in fast-paced or high-growth organizations. Deep understanding of audience engagement, community dynamics, and organic growth channels. Strong collaboration and communication skills with the ability to work effectively across product, marketing, and design teams. Analytical mindset with the ability to leverage qualitative insights and customer behavior data to inform creative decisions. Comfortable operating independently in a hands-on individual contributor role with significant ownership and autonomy. Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience preferred. Portfolio demonstrating creative writing, lifecycle campaigns, and brand storytelling experience required. Benefits Competitive base salary ranging from CA$125,000 to CA$175,000 annually depending on experience, skills, and location. Equity opportunities as part of the total compensation package. Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage. RRSP plan with employer matching contributions. Paid time off and paid parental leave programs. Wellness benefits and flexible remote work environment. Access to professional growth opportunities within a high-growth technology company. Collaborative and innovation-driven culture with significant ownership and impact. How Jobgether Works We use an AI-powered matc

title: Creative Director
description: For decades, DTN has been the silent force behind some of the world’s most critical industries—helping businesses navigate complexity, uncertainty, and risk with smarter, faster decisions. From agriculture to energy to weather intelligence, our proprietary Operational Decisioning Platform transforms raw data into decision-grade insights—enabling companies to optimize supply chains, ensure market stability, and safeguard infrastructure against disruption. We don’t follow trends—we set the standard for precision, trust, and operational impact. DTN is at an exciting inflection point. Building off a foundation of financial strength, profitability, and industry trust, we’re accelerating growth and expanding our global footprint. Our purpose-built solutions—powered by AI and honed by decades of vertical expertise—are helping some of the world’s most significant enterprises thrive amid operational constraints and uncover new opportunities in a fast-changing world. J ob Description: We are currently seeking a supremely skilled Creative Director to spearhead and mentor the DTN internal Creative Team while elevating DTN creative across all customer touchpoints. You will oversee the development and execution of every creative strategy, program and asset - including design, video, copywriting, website design and development while supporting three distinct business units and a diverse portfolio of solutions. You will report to the Chief Marketing Officer and manage a cross-disciplinary team of designers, writers, video producers, and developers, as well as select agency partners. Key Responsibilities Lead the In-House Creative Team: Recruit, mentor, and develop a team of designers, writers, and video/content creators, fostering a culture of innovation, collaboration, and high performance. Develop best-in-class creative for a variety of touchpoints - including but not limited to - design, brand, web, social, events, product, interface and more. Help lead and manage both in-house and external teams in creative strategy, execution and production. Creative Strategy & Vision: Own and drive the overall creative vision that aligns with company and business unit strategies, ensuring all campaigns are on-brand and on-message. Ai/Creative integration: Be comfortable with and able to work across several different Ai platforms from a creative and workflow perspective. Campaign Development: Develop and execute integrated creative campaigns for product launches, demand generation, digital marketing, sales enablement, events, and brand awareness across channels (web, social, email, video, print, etc.). Brand Consistency: Safeguard and evolve our brand standards, ensuring consistent messaging, tone, and visual identity across all touchpoints. Creative Management: Oversee creative project workflows, from initial concept through execution and delivery. Comfortable managing budgets and resources for maximum impact. Collaborative Partner: Work closely with marketing, sales, product, and executive teams to translate business objectives into compelling creative strategies. Data-Driven Approach: Leverage creative performance data and market insights to continuously improve effectiveness and drive ROI for marketing assets. Vendor Management: Oversee external creative vendors and agencies, ensuring alignment with internal capabilities and brand guidelines. Process Optimization: Implement scalable creative processes and best practices to improve quality, speed, and collaboration across the organization. Foster a collaborative and inclusive approach to your work, enabling cross team working to deliver the very best results Qualifications 10+ years of creative leadership experience in a B2B software, SaaS, or technology environment. Brings clarity to complexity through the power of storytelling - able to communicate intricate ideas with simplicity and impact. Proven track record overseeing high-performing creative teams delivering design, video, copywriting, and web
score_reason: Senior CD role reporting to CMO with team leadership and integrated creative scope, but no explicit remote/location details and missing salary info create uncertainty.

title: Creative Director, Event Design
description: Let’s cut to it. We’re after the kind of Creative Director who isn’t just “creative,” but creative in a way that’s wired straight into the zeitgeist. Someone who knows the difference between a good idea and a culture-shifting one, and who knows how to get it made. You’re a strategist’s strategist, a leader’s leader, and the person people trust to turn chaos into clarity. You’ve helmed experience end-to-end, built teams that punch above their weight, and still manage to make the room lighter and brighter when you walk in. You’re the benchmark others measure against. Why GMR? Because we’re not like the rest, and it’s not just something we say in pitch decks. We’re ingenious, flat by design, and allergic to big egos (ours or yours). We believe in ruthless transparency and radical support. We root for each other. We fight for the greatest ideas because it’s what’s right. We trade credit for collaboration and status for momentum, and right now, we’ve got more of that than ever. If you read this and think yes to all of this, it's time to talk. You might just do your best work here, and we’re set to let you fly. Please submit your portfolio or samples of your work to be considered for this role. ABOUT GMR MARKETING We are the Experience Agency Making Unforgettable Stories Born of Humanity NOT JUST EXPERIENTIAL. EXPERIENCE. The lines have blurred. People live in the physical, digital, and social worlds all at once, and your brand experiences should do the same. NOT JUST TELLING. MAKING. The best stories are lived, not heard. Make an immersive world for people to explore so they can experience something they’ll never forget. NOT JUST CONSUMERS. HUMANS. It all starts with the human. If you want to make a lasting impact, look beyond the wallet to understand what motivates and inspires people. REQUIRED SKILLS Lead creative that disrupts, not decorates. You’ll shape ideas from a blank page to award-winning, business-shifting campaigns. If it doesn’t make people talk, it’s not done yet. Be the insight hunter. You turn data, culture, and human behavior into creative gold by partnering with strategists to find the signal in the noise. Pitch like a legend. You own the room, turning concepts into must-buys and clients into believers, first meeting or a final sell. Accelerate from brief to brilliance. Your speed isn’t just about moving fast; it’s about moving smart, transforming briefs into work that sets new standards. Steer diverse creative experiences. Digital, social, experiential, emerging tech are in your vernacular. You lead teams and projects across every channel, every platform, every audience. Build strategic client relationships. You’re a problem-solver, a trusted advisor, and a creative diplomat that is always building and defending the kind of partnerships that make great work possible. Architect the team. You design team structures that balance talent, ambition, and sanity. You make sure the right people are in the right roles for maximum impact. Set the bar and raise it. You inspire, motivate, and demand excellence, creating a culture where great work is the baseline, not the exception. Give feedback that actually helps. You’re clear, direct, and real-time with your feedback which grows talent, not just critiquing it. Create a space for brilliance. Collaboration isn’t just a buzzword here at GMR. It’s how you build the environment where creativity thrives. Coach, mentor, elevate. You watch, encourage, and push your team to be better every day, ensuring consistency, growth, and a bit of magic in every project. Never stop learning. Curiosity isn’t optional. You’re relentlessly expanding your skills, your vision, and your impact. INCLUSION, BELONGING & IMPACT Our workplace is for ALL. We foster a culture where every background and perspective is valued so we can create meaningful memories that resonate with everyone. We aim to cultivate belonging by meeting people where they are, removing obstacles, and building on their strengths to
score_reason: Senior creative leadership in experiential/event design with strong cultural mandate, but no mention of remote work, compensation, or geographic flexibility—critical gaps for Travis's Costa Rica move.

title: Freelance Creative Director, Live Event & Experiential
description: Join a dynamic team as a Freelance Creative Director, where you'll lead the creative vision for a major tech event across wayfinding, scenic & spatial design, keynotes, and graphics. This role offers the opportunity to shape immersive experiences and collaborate with top-tier professionals in the industry. Responsibilities Lead the creative development of attendee journeys and arrival experiences, ensuring a memorable entrance and tunnel design. Oversee and refine scenic and environmental designs, collaborating closely with 3D designers and production teams for seamless execution. Guide creative direction across executive summits, including lounge and check-in experiences, environmental branding, and overall atmosphere. Direct the development of content and brand moments, ensuring cohesive integration across spaces. Provide creative direction for keynote motion and graphics packages, including theme loops, transitions, and stage visuals. Skills Proven experience in experiential design and live events, with a focus on shaping attendee journeys and physical environments. Strong expertise in motion and keynote presentations, with the ability to work across spatial, graphic, and content systems. Hands-on and collaborative approach, capable of stepping into ongoing projects and contributing effectively. Experience working with enterprise or tech clients, enhancing the ability to deliver tailored creative solutions. At Horizontal, we are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive environment where all individuals are valued and empowered to succeed. By applying for this position, you acknowledge and agree that Horizontal Talent may contact you regarding your application using automated technology, including phone calls, SMS/text messages, or email, which may be delivered by our virtual AI recruiter, Alex. Please apply through this online posting or by visiting our Job Board at www.horizontaltalent.com/job-board. Applications will be accepted for 4 weeks. For those that join the team, we offer competitive compensation and benefits including medical, dental, vision, and retirement. Check out all we have to offer and how you can become part of the Horizontal Talent Team. The pay range for this role is $75 - $80 per hour based on qualifications and experience.
score_reason: Senior experiential creative leadership with strong scope, but freelance structure, no salary stated, and unclear remote/location flexibility limit fit.

title: Chief Marketing Officer
description: About Us We are a staffing services technology company that helps organizations design, build, and scale digital products and engineering capabilities. Our teams deliver end-to-end software development, engineering, and design services, and we provide flexible staffing solutions to augment internal teams with specialized talent—quickly and reliably. The Role We are seeking a visionary, results-driven Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) to join our executive leadership team. You’ll be responsible for owning the brand story, driving customer acquisition, and leading our global marketing strategy. You’ll collaborate with Sales, Product, and the CEO to align marketing initiatives with revenue targets, ensuring a cohesive customer journey from brand awareness to advocacy. What You’ll Do Brand Strategy & Positioning: Define and execute the company’s long-term brand vision; oversee messaging, visual identity, and market positioning to ensure our value proposition resonates with target audiences and differentiates us from competitors. Demand Generation & Growth: Lead the strategy for inbound and outbound marketing channels; oversee performance marketing, SEO/SEM, content marketing, and events to drive high-quality leads and measurable revenue growth. Product Marketing & Go-to-Market: Orchestrate successful product launches; work with Product teams to translate technical features into compelling benefits and ensure sales teams are equipped with the right enablement tools. Customer Lifecycle & Retention: Develop strategies to increase customer lifetime value (CLV); oversee customer engagement programs, loyalty initiatives, and communications that foster retention and reduce churn. Collaboration: Work closely with the Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) and Sales leadership to align on lead definitions, pipeline targets, and conversion strategies; partner with Product to infuse customer feedback into the roadmap. Administrative & Reporting: Manage the marketing budget and resource allocation; establish a data-driven culture by tracking KPIs (CAC, LTV, ROAS, MQL/SQL conversion) and presenting performance reports to the Board of Directors. Market Intelligence & Innovation: Monitor industry trends, competitive landscapes, and consumer behavior; identify new market opportunities and leverage emerging technologies (e.g., AI in marketing) to maintain a competitive edge. What We’re Looking For Experience: 10+ years of progressive marketing leadership experience, with at least 3-5 years in a VP or C-level role; experience scaling B2B SaaS or high-growth consumer brands is highly preferred. Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field; MBA is a strong plus. Communication: Exceptional storytelling and public speaking skills; ability to inspire internal teams and represent the brand externally to media, analysts, and investors. Leadership: Proven ability to build, mentor, and lead a diverse marketing organization across multiple disciplines (Creative, Demand Gen, Comms, Product Marketing). Strategic Acumen: Demonstrated success in connecting marketing activities to the bottom line; ability to balance brand-building with short-term performance targets. Problem Solving: Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data sets to optimize spend and pivot strategies based on market feedback. Systems & Tools: Proficiency with the modern marketing stack (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce, Marketo, Google Analytics) and familiarity with attribution modeling. Resilience & Adaptability: Strategic thinker who is creative yet pragmatic; comfortable navigating rapid growth and changing market dynamics. Team Collaboration: Collaborative executive presence; comfortable breaking down silos between Sales, Product, and Marketing to drive unified business goals Compensation & Benefit We believe in paying top-of-market rates for top-tier talent. The base salary range for this role is $275,000 to $325,000, with exact placement determined by your
score_reason: Senior CMO title fits Core, but B2B staffing tech lacks creative DNA—this is demand-gen/revenue-focused marketing, not brand/creative leadership that plays to Travis's integrated campaign and creative department strength.

title: VP Brand Marketing & Operations
description: Claire’s is an iconic, beloved retail brand, best known as a go-to trend spot for kids, tween and teen girls and America’s top piercing destination, piercing over 130 Million ears over the past forty years. With its 900 retail locations across the US & Canada and a fresh new brand strategy, we are ready to enter a new era and are on a mission to build an inspiring playground for modern girlhood. With a clear remit to evolve the brand to meet the needs of Gen Alpha, we are transforming the brand to continue to be the beloved home of girlhood for generations to come. Join us in helping us realize this ambition and continue to make space and champion girlhood. Our Mission: Build an inspiring playground for modern girlhood. Our Beliefs: Girlhood is sacred. It deserves its own space. Give her the freedom to figure it out. Moments matter (and they’re better together) Our Vision: To build a world where girlhood is embraced, revered, and celebrated. About the Role Claire’s is building a next-generation brand at the intersection of culture, community, and commerce. We are seeking a VP, Brand Marketing & Operations to serve as the brand marketing operator behind this transformation. This is a critical role to the Chief Brand Officer- responsible for translating brand vision, creative direction, and partnerships into scalable, high-impact marketing programs. You will lead building the systems, processes, and cross-functional alignment required to deliver best-in-class campaigns, launches, and partnerships. This role is designed for a leader who builds engines, not just campaigns- someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, a builder, and knows how to turn ambition into disciplined execution at scale. This role ensures that strong insights- whether a campaign idea, collaboration, or cultural moment, are translated into a commercially impactful, flawlessly executed program. What You Will Own Go-To-Market Strategy & Launch Orchestration Own end-to-end GTM strategy and execution for product launches, campaigns, and key brand moments Lead annual brand planning, translating business objectives into compelling brand marketing strategies, seasonal storytelling, and major cultural moments. Develop integrated launch plans across paid, owned, earned, retail, and experiential channels Partner with Merchandising, Product, and Commercial teams to align product priorities with marketing execution Ensure all initiatives have: Clear objectives Defined KPIs Full-funnel amplification plans Build repeatable GTM frameworks that scale across categories, seasons, and channels Marketing Operations & Brand Engine Own the end-to-end marketing operating system- planning, briefing, GTM, execution, and post-mortems Build scalable processes that enable speed, clarity, and accountability across teams Lead core operating rhythms (campaign calendars, GTM reviews, launch readiness, weekly execution reviews) Ensure seamless orchestration across brand, retail, PR, social, and partnerships Translate brand vision into clear roadmaps, timelines, and deliverables Campaign Commercialization, Talent Activation & Team Operational Excellence Own the end-to-end commercialization of campaigns once creative direction and talent/creator partnerships are defined Translate campaign concepts into fully built, executable programs across channels, retail, and partners Lead development of: Detailed campaign plans and execution roadmaps Budget allocation and management across all channels and activations Production timelines, deliverables, and rollout sequencing Oversee key programs across: Influencer & creator programs PR & communications Events and retail activations Partner-driven campaigns and collaborations Lead Creative, PR, and Partnership teams to: Sharpen messaging, narrative, and rationale for campaigns and collaborations Ensure ideas are both compelling and executable at scale Act as the quality control layer across all campaigns and programs- ensuring work is delivered on tim

title: VP, Marketing & Growth
description: Who We Are Goodway Group is one of AdAge's 2025's BEST PLACES TO WORK ! As an independent and remote-first media and marketing services firm with a 90+ year history, Goodway Group has the security of an established company combined with a start-up feel. With leading data-driven and technology-enabled digital media and marketing services firm with teams in the U.S. and the UK, our diverse team of digital strategists, media practitioners, technologists and data scientists have won the most prestigious awards for innovative marketing technology, impactful work and inclusive remote-first places to work including being honored as a multiyear winner in Ad Age Best Places to Work, Ad Exchanger's Best Use of Technology by an Agency Award and two MarTech Breakthrough Awards and a certified service partner to The Trade Desk. Who You Are Goodway Group is looking for a strategic, commercially minded VP, Marketing & Growth to lead how we show up in market and how marketing contributes to growth. This person will own Goodway’s positioning, external narrative, reputational strategy, sales enablement, demand generation, and the evolution of the marketing operating model. This is not a traditional brand or communications role. It requires a senior leader who can shape the story, build the engine behind it, and translate marketing into proof, pipeline, and commercial impact. The role will partner closely with the COO, CEO, Business Development, and line-of-business leaders to create a more unified market presence for Goodway, while ensuring Overline continues to be marketed appropriately as a distinct consulting brand. This leader will also help modernize how marketing operates by introducing clearer systems, stronger prioritization, AI-enabled workflows, and scalable delivery models that may include external partners and offshore resources. The goal is to build a lean, high-performing marketing function that is credible in market, useful to the commercial teams, and designed to scale. What You Will Do Own and evolve Goodway Group’s positioning, messaging, and market narrative across brand, PR, thought leadership, events, and executive visibility. Build and lead a commercial marketing engine that strengthens sales enablement, improves pitch quality, and creates a repeatable stream of proof points, case studies, testimonials, and market-facing content. Partner closely with Business Development, leadership, and line-of-business teams to align marketing activity with growth priorities, pipeline needs, and a more unified “One Goodway” market presence. Lead demand generation and reputational marketing efforts that increase awareness, consideration, and qualified inbound opportunity across Goodway’s priority markets. Assess and evolve the marketing operating model, including team structure, workflows, tools, partner mix, and the use of AI and offshore production capabilities to improve speed, quality, and efficiency. Establish stronger marketing systems, operating rhythms, and prioritization, so the function is focused, measurable, and tied to business outcomes rather than reactive activity. What You Bring Significant senior marketing leadership experience in a B2B, agency, consulting, media, retail media, commerce, martech, or complex services business. Proven success in positioning, commercial storytelling, demand generation, and building marketing programs that support growth and revenue outcomes. Strong experience creating sales enablement and market-facing materials that are actually used by commercial teams. Deep understanding of how agency or services businesses operate, including the realities of people-led delivery models, utilization, and commercial support. Experience leading lean teams and using a mix of internal resources, agencies, freelancers, AI tools, and ideally offshore or distributed delivery models to scale output effectively. Strong executive presence, stakeholder management skills, and the ability to operate strategically while
score_reason: Senior remote-first role with strong positioning/narrative scope, but growth/demand-gen focus and B2B services positioning are outside Travis's integrated creative advertising DNA; no salary posted and US/UK footprint lacks explicit LATAM/worldwide clarity.

title: VP, Marketing (Remote)
description: We believe in the power and joy of learning At Cengage, our employees have a direct impact in helping students around the world discover the power and joy of learning. We are bonded by our shared purpose – driving innovation that helps millions of learners improve their lives and achieve their dreams through education. Focusing on college and career readiness, Cengage School equips students with the skills and confidence to succeed beyond the classroom. Through exclusive access to National Geographic Learning, Big Ideas Learning, Gale, Thorndike Press and Cengage curriculum and technology, it engages learners in core subjects and career pathways—empowering educators and inspiring student success. Our culture values inclusion, engagement, and discovery Our business is driven by our strong culture, and we know that creating an inclusive workplace is absolutely essential to the success of our company and our learners, as well as our individual well-being. We recognize the value of diverse perspectives in everything we do, and strive to ensure employees of all levels and backgrounds feel empowered to voice their ideas and bring their authentic selves to work. We achieve these priorities through programs, benefits, and initiatives that are integrated into the fabric of how we work every day. To learn more, please see https://www.cengagegroup.com/about/inclusion-and-belonging/. The Vice President, Marketing is aligned to the Cengage Learning’s K‑12 business, including the National Geographic Learning brand. This senior leader is jointly responsible for defining and implementing an integrated go‑to‑market strategy grounded in Product Marketing, Demand Generation, and Engagement Marketing. The role synthesizes market, customer, and performance insights to shape strategy and partners cross‑functionally to deliver a cohesive, differentiated story across products, audiences, and channels. A strategic problem solver with a strong execution orientation, this role leads teams that drive brand affinity, generate and convert demand, deepen customer engagement, and optimize the buyer and educator journey—while rigorously measuring impact and return on investment. What You'll Do Here Product Marketing (Positioning, Messaging & GTM) Lead product marketing strategy across the K‑12 portfolio, including messaging, positioning, and go‑to‑market execution. Use a research approach to uncover customer needs, product value, and competitive differentiation; translate insights into clear, compelling narratives. Partner with Product, Strategy, and Technology leaders to inform roadmap, packaging, pricing, and launch priorities. Provide executive insights to influence the development of new products, solutions, and services. Ensure consistent, differentiated storytelling across sales, marketing, brand, and customer touchpoints. Demand Marketing (Pipeline Growth & Conversion) Own marketing’s contribution to pipeline and revenue, using standard demand generation methodologies. Partner closely with Sales, GTM Operations, and Analytics to set, track, and achieve short‑ and long‑term pipeline goals. Lead high‑impact account‑based marketing (ABM) strategies focused on priority K‑12 districts, states, and adoption cycles. Optimize win rates and reduce objections through targeted campaigns, journey optimization, and sales enablement. Build attribution discipline and performance dashboards to ensure accountability and impact across programs. Engagement Marketing (Brand, Lifecycle & Thought Leadership) Enhance brand affinity and engagement through coordinated campaigns, content, thought leadership, and storytelling. Strong understanding of healthcare compliance frameworks, including FDA, OIG, HIPAA, and pharmaceutical industry ethical guidelines. Proven ability to lead complex projects, manage competing priorities, and operate effectively in fast-paced, evolving environments. Demonstrated expertise in cross-functional collaboration, stakeholder management, and influencing within matrix organizations. Excellent communication, presentation, coaching, and interpersonal skills with the ability to engage scientific and clinical audiences at all levels. Experience developing scientific content, educational tools, and training programs for customer-facing medical teams is highly valued. Knowledge of neuroscience, disease management, medical research practices, managed care dynamics, or field medical operations is considered a strong advantage. Previous field medical or Medical Science Liaison experience is preferred. Benefits Competitive salary ranging from $204,400 to $379,600 annually, based on experience and qualifications. Partner cross-functionally with Revenue Marketing, Web, Brand Strategy, PR/Comms, Product Marketing, Product, Data, and Investor Relations to ship integrated programs. Apply AI-first strategies to content production, distribution, LLM visibility, and audience intelligence, improving speed, consistency, and the quality of every asset shipped.
